description: Returns the list of supported GDAL raster drivers and file formats.
Reads a raster file (or a mosaic of raster files) and returns a table with the raster data. Supports filter pushdown on non-BLOB columns.
Exports the data table to a new raster file.
example: COPY ( SELECT geometry, databand_1 FROM RT_Read('path/to/raster/file.tif') ) TO 'path/to/raster/newfile.tif' WITH (FORMAT RASTER, DRIVER 'COG'...);

description: Transforms an array of numeric values into a databand column (BLOB) with the internal structure required by the raster extension. This is the inverse of RT_Cube2Array.
example: SELECT RT_Array2Cube(r.values, 'RAW', r.bands, r.cols, r.rows, r.no_data) FROM ...;

description: Transforms the BLOB data of the data band columns into an array of a numeric data type. Optionally filters out NoData values from the resulting array. A different function variant is provided for each numeric data type (e.g. RT_Cube2ArrayInt32, RT_Cube2ArrayFloat, etc.).
example: SELECT RT_Cube2ArrayInt32(databand_1, true) AS r FROM RT_Read('path/to/raster/file.tif');

description: Transforms a datacube column to another data type, converting the data buffer accordingly. A different function variant is provided for each numeric data type (e.g. RT_Cube2TypeUInt8, RT_Cube2TypeFloat, etc.).
Returns a data cube with the absolute value of each cell. No-data cells are preserved.
Returns a data cube with each cell equal to the sum of the corresponding cells of the two inputs. Inputs can be two data cubes or a data cube and a scalar. No-data cells are preserved.
Returns a data cube where cells inside the given geometry are replaced by the specified value. Cells outside the geometry are preserved. No-data cells are preserved.
example: SELECT RT_CubeBurn(databand_1, tile_x, tile_y, blocksize_x, blocksize_y, geo_transform, ST_GeomFromText('POLYGON((...))'), 255.0) FROM ...;

description: Returns a data cube where cells outside the given geometry are replaced by the specified value. Cells inside the geometry are preserved. No-data cells are preserved.
example: SELECT RT_CubeClip(databand_1, tile_x, tile_y, blocksize_x, blocksize_y, geo_transform, ST_GeomFromText('POLYGON((...))'), 0.0) FROM ...;

description: Returns a data cube with each cell equal to the left-hand cell divided by the right-hand cell. Inputs can be two data cubes or a data cube and a scalar. No-data cells are preserved.
Returns a data cube where each cell is 1 if the corresponding cells of the two inputs are equal, 0 otherwise. Inputs can be two data cubes or a data cube and a scalar. No-data cells are preserved.
Returns a data cube with the exponential (e^x) of each cell. No-data cells are preserved.
Returns a data cube where all cells (including no-data) are unconditionally replaced by the corresponding values of datacube_b or a scalar, bypassing no-data checks.

Returns a data cube where each cell is 1 if the left-hand cell is strictly greater than the right-hand cell, 0 otherwise. Inputs can be two data cubes or a data cube and a scalar. No-data cells are preserved.

Returns a data cube where each cell is 1 if the left-hand cell is greater than or equal to the right-hand cell, 0 otherwise. Inputs can be two data cubes or a data cube and a scalar. No-data cells are preserved.

Returns a data cube where each cell is 1 if the left-hand cell is strictly less than the right-hand cell, 0 otherwise. Inputs can be two data cubes or a data cube and a scalar. No-data cells are preserved.

Returns a data cube where each cell is 1 if the left-hand cell is less than or equal to the right-hand cell, 0 otherwise. Inputs can be two data cubes or a data cube and a scalar. No-data cells are preserved.
Returns a data cube with the natural logarithm of each cell. No-data cells are preserved.
Returns a data cube with each cell equal to the maximum of the corresponding cells of datacube_a and datacube_b or a scalar value. No-data cells are preserved.
Returns a data cube with each cell equal to the minimum of the corresponding cells of datacube_a and datacube_b or a scalar value. No-data cells are preserved.
Returns a data cube with each cell equal to the remainder of dividing the left-hand cell by datacube_b or a scalar value element-wise. No-data cells are preserved.
Returns a data cube with each cell equal to the product of the corresponding cells of the two inputs. Inputs can be two data cubes or a data cube and a scalar. No-data cells are preserved.
Returns a data cube with each cell negated (multiplied by -1). No-data cells are preserved.
Returns a data cube where each cell is 1 if the corresponding cells of the two inputs differ, 0 otherwise. Inputs can be two data cubes or a data cube and a scalar. No-data cells are preserved.
Creates a polygon geometry for each contiguous region of non-no-data values in the data cube.
example: SELECT RT_CubePolygonize(databand_1, tile_x, tile_y, blocksize_x, blocksize_y, geo_transform) FROM ...;

description: Returns a data cube with each cell raised to the power of the corresponding right-hand cell. Inputs can be two data cubes or a data cube and a scalar. No-data cells are preserved.
Returns a data cube where valid cells are replaced by the corresponding values of datacube_b or a scalar. No-data cells in the source are preserved.
Returns a data cube where nodata cells are replaced by the specified value, and sets this value as the new nodata sentinel.
Returns a data cube with the square root of each cell. No-data cells are preserved.
Calculates statistics for a specific band (0-based index) of a data cube. Returns a STRUCT with minimum, maximum, mean, stddev, valid_count and nodata_count fields.
example: SELECT RT_CubeStats(databand_1, 0) AS stats FROM RT_Read('path/to/raster/file.tif');

description: Returns a data cube with each cell equal to the left-hand cell minus the right-hand cell. Inputs can be two data cubes or a data cube and a scalar. No-data cells are preserved.
